page: Microsoft Azure E8-2ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Eadsv7 family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU.

For local storage, the instance includes a 472 GB local disk and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. There are no graphics processing units bundled with this configuration, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The high memory density relative to the low vCPU count makes this instance a specialized option for memory-bound workloads that do not require extensive parallel processing power. Qualitatively, this resource allocation offers cost efficiency for applications needing large memory footprints without the expense of licensing or provisioning excess CPU cores. Tradeoffs include limited compute scaling due to the two-vCPU limit and the lack of hardware acceleration. This configuration is suitable for small to medium database hosting, in-memory caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-2ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory, which provides a high ratio of 32.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by a 472 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration. The high memory-to-CPU ratio makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that require minimal compute resources, avoiding the need to pay for unneeded vCPUs. It is suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
